All right, now we are going to go into challenge manager. So this person, challenge manager, accountability coach, the term will be kind of used interchangeably, but this is the person that is going to be responsible for everybody that's on a six-week, 28-day, 12-week challenge promotional period before their regular member, month to month. So this person needs to be really organized, especially if you're doing rolling starts. So like you're starting a new group every Monday or Saturday, you'll essentially have six groups running at the same time that are all at different points during the challenge. So they need to be really organized at who's at what point to make sure that everybody that's signing up is showing up to their weigh-ins. Everybody that is showing up to their weigh-ins is purchasing supplements. So this person should be pretty good at sales if that is who you have due to sales. Otherwise you can have the owner do it. They should be also scheduling the halfway meetings and making sure that the person shows up to the halfway meetings. They should also be scheduling the weigh-outs and making sure that they are showing up to the weigh-outs. Their main goal is to convert everybody from a six-week challenge to a regular recurring EFT member by just over-delivering, being very organized, helping them with problems related to non-results. Accountability is huge. Tracking their weekly weigh-ins, tracking their visits, sending them messages based on both of those that are unique to the person using their name, super friendly. And so when it gets to the point of having, let's say you sign up 10, let's say you sign up 15 new challengers a week. So that's going to be 60 by the end of the month, but you'll have two weeks that are still left over from the previous month of challengers. So now you're at 80. I'm sorry, 90. So you have 90 challengers that are at any given time. That's a lot of work for the owner to do while they're doing all the rest of the stuff. So that's when you enter this position is when you are getting your challenges to the point, the challenges to the point that you're signing up so many people that it's really difficult to actually give good service to the challengers. That's when you dedicate somebody to it because a difference of, let's say 50% conversion with 70% conversion, if you have somebody dedicated to only working the challengers, is that 20% if you did the math over a $200 EFT is going to be a fair amount. It would be roughly you're saving 20 more challengers. It's $4,000 a month in new EFTs that you would be losing if you lost the extra percentage that you just couldn't retain because your fulfillment wasn't that good. But with somebody that's dedicated to it, your fulfillment should be really good. And that's exactly what this person should be doing. So how to train them is in the portal and the downloadable portal and exactly what they should be doing every single day is in there as well. Therefore, ours document is in a clickable button. This document here that goes, it's just a summary of their duties is downloadable as well. But you really want to find the right person that has a knowledge of fitness and nutrition because they will be talking with members on a regular basis about how the challenge is going and their results and everything like that. But it's also very highly organized and can keep track of everybody because it's a lot of moving people all super excited because they just started their challenge. So keeping these groups very organized, knowing who needs to go to weigh in, who has already attended, who's already purchased, who needs to be pitched, all of that stuff is really important for your challenge manager. So as your gym scales and you get better at sales, that means more challengers. You have a lot of people, this is the time that you should hire the person. So take the time, invest the time to make the portal your own. So if your challenge manager doesn't work out, you have all the training ready to rock and roll for the next person and you can get going.
